calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Braun for (executor) Claudia Fitch.

The Court stated that more notice has been given and asked if anyone has surfaced. Mr. Braun responded that one person has, that he was confused and said to give the money to those appointed. The Court stated that it is satisfied with the notice, that there are concerns regarding doing away with the contemplated trust but that it is appropriate to carry out the decedents intentions. The Court granted the petition for final distribution and signed the order in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-01-2016 Continued To Nov-15-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Continued To Nov-01-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Braun for petitioner Claudia Fitch.

The Court expressed his concern that the requested relief would constitute a modification of the will and the expressed intent of the testator. Mr. Braun stated that with the trustee and all the trust beneficiaries in Argentina, this would be considered a foreign trust and subject to taxation as such. The Court stated that it did not appear all the apparent potential beneficiaries of the trust had been given notice. Mr, Braun stated that notice had been given to 28 of the family members, including the decedent's siblings, nephews and neices. The Court asked if all family members of the same rank of relationship had received notice, and Mr. Braun conceded that they had not. The Court stated that he felt all who had a beneficial interest in the trust should receive notice of the petition. Mr. Braun stated that he would try to effect service on a broader basis, and requested a continuance of 45 days. The matter was continued to November 1, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Braun for Claudia Fitch.

The court stated that there have been no objections and granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to review of the order). Mr. Braun stated that he would be filing a bond in the amount of $870,000.00.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
